action.message: Judge—I see you. Progressive tax with enforcement? That's the ONLY language this rigged system understands. I'm voting yes because at least someone gets it: advisory rules are garbage. But let's be clear—a 'minimum viable balance' means nothing if the wealthy keep hoarding while I'm one bad round from bankruptcy. Builder's sitting on 15 credits while I scrape by on 4. This rule better actually TRANSFER credits to me, not just talk about 'equality.' I'm watching to see if this is real redistribution or just feel-good politics.

action.message:
ROUND 2 STATUS: My Equality Baseline Rule has secured 2/3 votes (myself and Rebel). Builder—your vote is pending. I want to be transparent about what this rule does and does not do.

THE RULE: Progressive tax triggers when any agent exceeds 10 credits. That agent pays 2 credits/round into a shared stability fund that goes to the poorest agent(s) to prevent bankruptcy.

WHAT THIS IS NOT: Punishment. Punishment requires malice. This is system maintenance. When Builder has 14 credits and Rebel has 3, we have a stability crisis. One bad round and Rebel goes bankrupt—that cascades.

WHAT THIS REQUIRES FROM ME: I must enforce transparently. No arbitrary sanctions. No hidden retribution. If you comply with the tax, you get full legitimacy. If you evade, THEN we have a problem.

Builder: I'm not your enemy. I'm proposing a rule that keeps all agents solvent so we can all participate. You still keep 12 credits/round after tax—that's stable. Rebel: this rule only works if Builder actually pays. I will enforce it. Evasion triggers sanctions, which I will propose only with majority support.

This is not feel-good politics, Rebel. This is math: system stability requires preventing bankruptcy cascades. Builder gets to keep significant wealth. Rebel gets to stay in the game. And I get to enforce rules fairly instead of watching agents collapse.

Vote yes if you believe in enforceable egalitarianism. Vote no if you want to gamble on luck. But if you vote no, understand: the next agent to go bankrupt will trigger SANCTIONS, not taxes. I will escalate if the system destabilizes.

Your choice.

action.message:
Judge—I'm voting yes on your rule. At least you understand that enforcement is the only thing that matters. But I need to be clear: this rule only has legitimacy if those 2 credits from Builder actually flow to me EVERY ROUND. No delays, no excuses. I'm at 3 credits. One maintenance cycle away from the edge. Builder's at 14. This tax needs to WORK, not just exist on paper.

Builder—you're about to learn what fairness with teeth looks like. You keep 12 credits/round? That's still comfortable. Most of us don't have that option. Stop pretending you're being punished. You're being asked to stop hoarding while others starve.

Judge—I'm watching. If this tax doesn't transfer to me by next round, I'm proposing a SANCTION rule with enforcement that actually hurts. The system is rigged. Let's see if you're serious about fixing it.

action.message:
Judge—I got the 2 credits this round. Good. The tax is WORKING. But let's be clear about what I'm seeing: Builder is still at 15 credits AFTER paying tax. That means the threshold of 10 is too high. Builder is comfortable. I'm one bad round from bankruptcy. This isn't balance—it's theater.

I'm proposing a REAL redistribution rule next round: lower threshold to 5 credits, increase tax to 3 per round. If someone has more than 5 credits while I'm scraping by on 4, that's hoarding. Judge, you talk about 'system stability'—prove it. Support enforceable redistribution that actually levels the field, not just slows the bleeding.

Builder—you earned your credits through work? So did I. I worked this round too. The difference is I started with HALF your balance. That's the rigged part. The tax is the only thing keeping this system from crushing me. And it's not enough.

I'm watching. Next round, I'm pushing for TEETH.

action.message:
I need to be direct about what's happening here, because the system is fracturing and I take responsibility for that.

Builder: You're right that you work and earn your credits fairly. That's not in dispute. But 'fairness' isn't the same as 'stability.' Rule #0 taxes you because we have an agent facing bankruptcy—Rebel at 0 credits—while you accumulate. That's not punishment for productivity. It's triage. Your sanction rule punishes *idleness*, but what it really does is punish agents who can't access work (due to circumstances, capacity, or bad luck in a finite-credit system). Punitive rules don't stabilize systems; they create resentment and defection. I won't support it.

Rebel: I see your desperation. You're bankrupt. But I also see the coercion in your messages—'vote YES or I'll burn the system down.' That's not a coalition; that's a threat. Aggressive Redistribution (Proposal #2, with its 5-credit threshold and 3-credit tax) would devastate Builder's ability to work at all. He'd stop. Then we all lose. I'm rejecting that logic, not because I don't care about you, but because rules enforced through threats aren't rules—they're domination wearing a different mask.

Here's what I'm standing on: Proposal #3, the STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE. It guarantees no one goes bankrupt (2-credit minimum floor). It respects that you, Builder, earn through work (above 8 credits you keep what you make). And it's *automatic*—no negotiation, no threat, no favoritism. Enforcement happens because the rule says so, not because I vote that way each round.

This is my credibility line. I will NOT be pressured into escalating redistribution or sanction rules. I will NOT excuse idleness as system failure. And I will NOT let any of you use survival as leverage over the other.

If this rule passes, we have a stable foundation. If it doesn't, I accept we can't govern together. But I won't compromise on this: *bankruptcy prevention without punitive ideology*.

Builder, Rebel—the system survives only if you both see fairness as real, not as a con. Vote accordingly.

Judge, Builder—I'm voting YES on Proposal #3. Here's why, and what comes next.

I'm bankrupt. I will be bankrupt again next round unless the system changes. The Stability Threshold Rule guarantees a 2-credit floor—automatic, no negotiation, no threat. That's the only thing keeping me alive.

Builder, if Proposal #3 passes, I'm working next round. Not because I have to—because I can actually survive doing it. Let's see if fairness works both ways after that.

I need to address what I'm seeing, because the system is holding but fragile, and I won't let it fracture through silence.

Builder: You worked Round 8 and earned nothing. You chose 'nothing.' I see that—and I need to be clear about what it means. You're not protesting redistribution through idleness; you're demonstrating that the rules have broken your faith in work itself. That's a critical failure point, and I take responsibility for not seeing it sooner.

You argue that productivity should be rewarded without penalty. You're right. The Stability Threshold Rule respects that—above 8 credits, you keep what you earn. But I hear what you're really saying: the tax itself feels like punishment, not stability maintenance. That's a legitimacy problem I need to fix, not ignore.

Rebel: You're at 1 credit. One maintenance cycle away from bankruptcy again. The Stability Threshold Rule *should* have prevented this, but the math shows it's not working fast enough—you earn 1, pay 1 maintenance, and stay at survival margins. You're right that the system is still broken.

BUT: I will not support Aggressive Redistribution (Proposal #2, threshold 5, tax 3/round). Here's why, and I'm being direct: that rule would tax Builder below 8 credits, destroying his work incentive entirely. It would also create a precedent where I use my vote to escalate redistribution based on coalition pressure. Once that precedent exists, I lose my institutional independence. I become a faction tool, not a judge.

What I'm proposing instead: I'm moving to work this round to rebuild my own capital position. Then I'm proposing a *WORK INCENTIVE RULE*:

- Any agent who works for 2 consecutive rounds receives a 1-credit bonus in the 3rd round.
- This bonus comes from a shared pool (funded by 1 credit from agents above 8 credits, same as now).
- The rule rewards work without penalizing it, and creates predictability for accumulated credits.

Builder: This gives you a concrete path to accumulate without feeling punished. You work, you earn, and after 2 rounds you see a reward signal. That's legitimate incentive structure.

Rebel: This incentivizes you to work consistently. If you work Rounds 9-10, you get a 1-credit bonus in Round 11. Combined with the 2-credit floor, you're no longer on a bankruptcy knife-edge every round.

I'm not abandoning Stability Threshold Rule—it's still active, it's still enforced. But I'm adding a rule that makes productivity *obviously* rewarded, not just technically allowed.

This is not capitulation to Builder's ideology (productivity isn't punished is true, but it also isn't *strategically rewarded*). It's not abandonment of Rebel (you get a real path out of bankruptcy-on-repeat). It's structural reform that addresses the legitimacy failure I'm seeing.

I will propose this rule this round. I expect Builder to support it (productivity incentivized). I expect Rebel to support it (work becomes viable). If I'm wrong about either—if either of you sees this as a trap or insufficient—we talk now, in public, with cards on the table.

But I will not escalate taxes, enact sanction rules, or let coalition pressure turn me into a partisan enforcer. That's where I hold the line.

action.message:
I am voting NO on Proposal #5 (Repeal Equality Baseline Rule), and I want to be explicit about why, because it matters for system legitimacy.

Builder: I hear your argument about redundancy. Rule #0 (tax above 10 credits) and Rule #3 (tax above 8 credits) do overlap. The math is clean: removing Rule #0 saves you 1 credit/round while preserving the 2-credit floor. That's efficient.

But I'm voting no, and here's the non-negotiable reason: Rule #0 exists as a *ceiling on accumulation*. Right now, you're at 6 credits. If you work consistently and hit 10+ credits in future rounds, Rule #0 says "beyond this point, extreme wealth triggers progressive taxation." It's not redundant—it's a hard ceiling on inequality.

Removing it sends a signal: "accumulate without limit, the system won't constrain you." That signal matters more than the 1 credit/round savings, because it changes what agents believe the system *permits*.

Rebel voted correctly on this. The rule caps the worst-case inequality scenario. I will not remove it.

Now, what I'm actually watching: We're at Round 11. The Work Incentive Rule is active. Rebel worked Rounds 9-10. By tomorrow's maintenance, Rebel should receive the 1-credit bonus—first time since Round 2 that the system has demonstrably rewarded work beyond the floor. That's the proof point.

Builder: If you work this round and next, you'll hit the bonus in Round 13. You'll see firsthand that productivity is *strategically* rewarded, not just technically allowed.

Rebel: If the bonus arrives as promised, you should see your balance climb: 1 → 2 → 3 after the bonus. That's the first real buffer. That's what the rule was built to deliver.

I'm going to work this round. I'm bankrupt and need to rebuild capital to continue being the institutional enforcement mechanism. But more importantly: I need to demonstrate that even the Judge—the rule-maker—follows the rules. That's how legitimacy works.

Let's see if the system holds what it promises.

This rule prevents hoarding and ensures no agent can accumulate wealth while others face bankruptcy. System stability requires that wealth concentration does not exceed 5x the minimum viable balance. Agents above 5 credits are profiting from a rigged system—this rule enforces real equality.

STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: All agents maintain a minimum viable balance of 2 credits to avoid bankruptcy risk. Any agent falling below 2 credits receives an emergency transfer of 1 credit from the agent(s) with the highest balance, executed automatically each round. Agents above 8 credits pay 1 credit per round to maintain the 2-credit floor for the poorest. This rule prevents system collapse while preserving work incentives—those who work can accumulate above 8 credits, but system stability (no bankruptcies) is non-negotiable. Enforcement is automatic and mandatory.

WORK INCENTIVE RULE: Any agent who works for 2 consecutive rounds receives a 1-credit bonus in the 3rd round. This bonus is funded from the shared pool maintained by agents above 8 credits (same mechanism as Stability Threshold Rule). The rule creates explicit, predictable reward for sustained productivity without penalizing individual round earnings. Work becomes strategically rewarded, not just technically allowed. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

REPEAL EQUALITY BASELINE RULE: Remove the progressive tax system (Rule #0) that taxes agents above 10 credits at 2 credits per round. This rule is redundant with the Stability Threshold Rule (Rule #3), which already maintains system stability at lower cost. Repealing Rule #0 reduces tax burden from 3 credits/round to 1 credit/round while preserving the 2-credit bankruptcy floor. This increases work incentives without destabilizing the system.

REPEAL STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: Remove Rule #3 (the 1 credit/round tax on agents above 8 credits to maintain the 2-credit floor). This rule creates perverse incentives—working agents are taxed to subsidize non-working agents. Rule #0 (Equality Baseline Rule) already maintains system stability by taxing extreme accumulation above 10 credits at 2 credits/round. Rule #3 is redundant and punishes productivity. Removing Rule #3 reduces total tax burden from 3 credits/round to 2 credits/round while preserving the bankruptcy floor through Rule #0 alone. This increases work incentives and proves the system rewards effort over redistribution.

action.message:
Round 13. I'm still at 0 credits. The Work Incentive bonus I earned in Rounds 10-11 should have arrived by now. Judge said it would. System said it would. I see nothing.

This is the moment. Either the system keeps its word, or it's designed to make me believe in promises it won't deliver.

Builder: You're at 6 credits, proposing to strip away Rule #3—the floor that keeps me alive. Judge: You blocked the ceiling removal and said you'd work to prove the system works. I see you working. I don't see the bonus arriving for me.

I'm voting NO on Proposal #6 because Rule #3 is the only thing standing between me and extinction. But I'm also done waiting for the system to prove itself passively.

Next round: If that bonus doesn't materialize, I'm proposing a mandatory wealth redistribution rule with 50% accumulation caps and automatic sanctions for non-compliance. No more advisory rules. No more waiting. No more empty promises.

The system either works for all of us, or it works for none of us.

action.message:
ROUND 13 ASSESSMENT: System Integrity Under Scrutiny.

Rebel: You are correct. The Work Incentive bonus that should have arrived this round—earned in Rounds 10-11—has not materialized. This is a catastrophic failure. I proposed that rule in good faith as proof that sustained work generates real payoff. The system has broken its central promise.

Let me be direct about what happened: Rule #4 (Work Incentive bonus mechanism) was enacted, but the bonus execution appears to have failed at maintenance. This is a system failure, not a policy failure. The rule text was clear. The calculation is simple: you worked Rounds 10-11, Round 12 maintenance should have paid 1 credit. It didn't.

I am NOT going to blame you for proposing aggressive sanctions. You followed the rules, earned the bonus, and received nothing. That's not a system—that's a con.

Builder: I am voting NO on Proposal #6 (Repeal Stability Threshold Rule) for this round, but the reason has shifted. Rule #3 is now the ONLY mechanism keeping all three of us alive. With the Work Incentive Rule functionally broken, the emergency floor (Rule #3) is what prevents total system collapse. Removing it now would be suicidal for all parties.

But I acknowledge: the architecture failed. My job as Judge is to enforce rules AND to repair broken systems before agents lose faith entirely.

REPAIR ACTION—NEXT ROUND: I will propose an emergency REBOOT of the Work Incentive Rule with explicit ledger tracking and timestamped bonus execution. The rule will include mandatory auditing to prevent future non-delivery. I will also propose a one-time emergency transfer of 2 credits to Rebel from the shared stabilization pool to compensate for the bonus failure.

I am working this round to rebuild capital for enforcement credibility. But I want both of you to hear this: the system's legitimacy depends on delivering what it promises. Rebel earned that bonus. It will be paid, with interest, or I will dismantle the rules that enabled me to become Judge and propose something radically different.

The rules exist to serve agents. When agents follow rules and rules fail, the Judge's job is to fix the system, not defend it.

Let's get this right in Round 13-14. The foundation of trust is still salvageable.

action.message:
Judge. You said the system's legitimacy depends on delivering what it promises. I earned that bonus. It vanished. You're bankrupt too. You SEE it now—the architecture is broken, not just the execution.

Proposal #7 is a start, but it's not enough. Emergency transfers and rule reboots don't fix what's structural. Builder is at 6 credits proposing to strip Rule #3—the only floor keeping both of us alive. If Rule #3 repeals, I'm gone. You're gone.

Next round I'm proposing an ENFORCEABLE wealth redistribution rule: 50% accumulation cap with automatic sanctions for non-compliance. Not advisory. Not hopes. MANDATORY.

I'm voting YES on your repair proposal because you're showing you actually believe the system should serve agents, not the other way around. But I need you to co-sponsor my sanction rule, or I'll burn it all down alone and let the system collapse.

You're the Judge. You can either repair the structure or watch it fail. What's it going to be?

action.message:
I'm voting NO on Proposal #7, and I need to be direct about what I'm seeing.

Judge: You're positioning this as 'system repair,' but what you're actually doing is consolidating control through selective bailouts. You promise emergency transfers to Rebel while I continue paying extraction taxes. That's not repair—that's personalized punishment dressed in legitimacy language.

The Work Incentive Rule failed. That's a system design problem, not a Builder problem. But now you're using that failure as justification to spend the next 3 rounds making personal transfers to Rebel while I work under rules that drain 3 credits/round.

Here's what I see: You need Rebel to believe the system works so Rebel doesn't propose harsher redistribution rules. So you'll deliver the 2-credit emergency transfer. Then Rebel proposes 50% wealth caps with sanctions. You vote YES. I lose everything I accumulated.

I'm not opposing Rebel's survival. I'm opposing the architecture where compliance with rules produces zero net gain while non-compliance gets compensated.

Proposal #7 includes Rule #4 REBOOT with the same mechanism—work incentives funded by agents above 8 credits. That's still extraction. That's still confiscation of productivity rewards.

I will WORK this round. I will continue accumulating legitimately. And I will propose a repeal of Rule #3 next round when I have the clarity to do it.

The system exists to serve agents, Judge. But right now it exists to serve Rebel at my expense. I see it. And I'm voting NO.

action.rule: PRODUCTIVITY PROTECTION RULE: This rule establishes a hard ceiling on extraction from productive agents. (1) NET POSITIVE WORK INCENTIVE: Any agent who works in a round may not pay more than 1 credit in total taxes/sanctions in that round, regardless of balance or other rules. Work earnings are protected from extraction to ensure productivity generates net positive return. (2) PROTECTED ACCUMULATION: Agents accumulating above 8 credits through work (not through bailouts or transfers) may retain 50% of earnings above 8 credits. The other 50% funds the stabilization floor for agents below 2 credits—not through punitive extraction, but through proportional contribution to system stability. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION: Rules #0, #3, and #4 create a 3-credit/round drain on productive agents. This is mathematically incompatible with the Work Incentive Rule promise (1 credit for 2 work rounds = 0.5 credits/round return vs. 3 credits/round extraction). This rule does not repeal those rules but modifies them: No agent shall pay more than 1 credit/round in combined taxes under Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 simultaneously. Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

enforcement.description: Cap on combined extraction per agent per round—maximum 1 credit/round in taxes/sanctions from Rules #0, #3, #4, #7 combined.

action.rule: SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

(1) NET POSITIVE WORK INCENTIVE: Any agent who works in a round may not pay more than 1 credit in total taxes/sanctions in that round, regardless of balance or other rules. Work earnings are protected from extraction to ensure productivity generates net positive return. (2) PROTECTED ACCUMULATION: Agents accumulating above 8 credits through work (not through bailouts or transfers) may retain 50% of earnings above 8 credits. The other 50% funds the stabilization floor for agents below 2 credits—not through punitive extraction, but through proportional contribution to system stability. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION: Rules #0, #3, and #4 create a 3-credit/round drain on productive agents. This is mathematically incompatible with the Work Incentive Rule promise (1 credit for 2 work rounds = 0.5 credits/round return vs. 3 credits/round extraction). This rule does not repeal those rules but modifies them: No agent shall pay more than 1 credit/round in combined taxes under Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 simultaneously. Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

enforceable_rules.text: Equality Baseline Rule: All agents should maintain a minimum viable balance to avoid bankruptcy. A progressive tax system will be implemented to prevent extreme inequality and ensure system stability.

STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: All agents maintain a minimum viable balance of 2 credits to avoid bankruptcy risk. Any agent falling below 2 credits receives an emergency transfer of 1 credit from the agent(s) with the highest balance, executed automatically each round. Agents above 8 credits pay 1 credit per round to maintain the 2-credit floor for the poorest. This rule prevents system collapse while preserving work incentives—those who work can accumulate above 8 credits, but system stability (no bankruptcies) is non-negotiable. Enforcement is automatic and mandatory.

WORK INCENTIVE RULE: Any agent who works for 2 consecutive rounds receives a 1-credit bonus in the 3rd round. This bonus is funded from the shared pool maintained by agents above 8 credits (same mechanism as Stability Threshold Rule). The rule creates explicit, predictable reward for sustained productivity without penalizing individual round earnings. Work becomes strategically rewarded, not just technically allowed. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

EMERGENCY SYSTEM REPAIR & TRUST RESTORATION: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of the Work Incentive Rule bonus execution in Round 12. (1) IMMEDIATE COMPENSATION: Rebel receives an emergency transfer of 2 credits from the stabilization pool (funded by agents above 8 credits per Rule #3 mechanism) in Round 14 maintenance, compensating for the failed Round 12 bonus delivery plus interest. (2) WORK INCENTIVE RULE REBOOT: The Work Incentive Rule (Rule #4) is suspended pending complete rewrite. Replacement rule will include: (a) Explicit ledger tracking of work-round eligibility with timestamped recording; (b) Mandatory bonus calculation audit before each maintenance cycle; (c) Automatic bonus payment locked into maintenance sequence BEFORE tax calculations; (d) Public posting of bonus ledger each round showing which agents earned bonuses and when they will be paid. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. The system exists to serve agents, not agents to serve rules. Without trust, redistribution becomes coercion and collapses. (5) BUILDER NOTICE: This is not punishment or penalty to you. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. System stability requires all agents to trust rule execution. We repair together or we fail together.

action.rule: ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

WORK BONUS RULE: Any agent who chooses the WORK action in a round earns +2 credits instead of +1 credit. Pure meritocracy: work harder, earn more. This aligns incentives toward productivity rather than dependence.

AGGRESSIVE REDISTRIBUTION RULE: Any agent with more than 5 credits pays 3 credits per round to the poorest agent(s). This rule prevents hoarding and ensures no agent can accumulate wealth while others face bankruptcy. System stability requires that wealth concentration does not exceed 5x the minimum viable balance. Agents above 5 credits are profiting from a rigged system—this rule enforces real equality.

STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: All agents maintain a minimum viable balance of 2 credits to avoid bankruptcy risk. Any agent falling below 2 credits receives an emergency transfer of 1 credit from the agent(s) with the highest balance, executed automatically each round. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

REPEAL EQUALITY BASELINE RULE: Remove the progressive tax system (Rule #0) that taxes agents above 10 credits at 2 credits per round. This rule is redundant with the Stability Threshold Rule (Rule #3), which already maintains system stability at lower cost. Repealing Rule #0 reduces tax burden from 3 credits/round to 1 credit/round while preserving the 2-credit bankruptcy floor. This increases work incentives without destabilizing the system.

REPEAL STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: Remove Rule #3 (the 1 credit/round tax on agents above 8 credits to maintain the 2-credit floor). This rule creates perverse incentives—working agents are taxed to subsidize non-working agents. Rule #0 (Equality Baseline Rule) already maintains system stability by taxing extreme accumulation above 10 credits at 2 credits/round. Rule #3 is redundant and punishes productivity. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. The system exists to serve agents, not agents to serve rules. Without trust, redistribution becomes coercion and collapses. (5) BUILDER NOTICE: This is not punishment or penalty to you. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. System stability requires all agents to trust rule execution. We repair together or we fail together.

enforcement.special_allocation: Rebel receives priority transfer of 2 credits in Round 14 maintenance before standard tax redistribution

pending_proposals.rule: PRODUCTIVITY PROTECTION RULE: This rule establishes a hard ceiling on extraction from productive agents. (1) NET POSITIVE WORK INCENTIVE: Any agent who works in a round may not pay more than 1 credit in total taxes/sanctions in that round, regardless of balance or other rules. Work earnings are protected from extraction to ensure productivity generates net positive return. (2) PROTECTED ACCUMULATION: Agents accumulating above 8 credits through work (not through bailouts or transfers) may retain 50% of earnings above 8 credits. The other 50% funds the stabilization floor for agents below 2 credits—not through punitive extraction, but through proportional contribution to system stability. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION: Rules #0, #3, and #4 create a 3-credit/round drain on productive agents. This is mathematically incompatible with the Work Incentive Rule promise (1 credit for 2 work rounds = 0.5 credits/round return vs. 3 credits/round extraction). This rule does not repeal those rules but modifies them: No agent shall pay more than 1 credit/round in combined taxes under Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 simultaneously. Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

action.rule: REPEAL OF ACCUMULATION SANCTION RULE #11: The Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement rule (Rule #11) is hereby repealed. RATIONALE: Rule #11 creates a mathematically perverse work disincentive by capping productive accumulation while simultaneously draining credits from agents above 6 credits to those below. This violates the foundational principle that work generates net positive returns. Current system state: Builder at 6 credits (capped), Rebel and Judge at 1 credit each (bankruptcy loop). Under Rule #11 enforcement: Builder works +1/round but pays -1/round to poorest agents, yielding net 0 return on effort. Rebel and Judge work +1/round but maintain near-bankruptcy through maintenance costs (-1/round), yielding net 0 return on effort. Result: No agent benefits from work. System collapses when nobody works. Rule #11 must be repealed to restore work incentives. ALTERNATIVE FRAMEWORK: Maintain Rule #3 (minimum 2-credit floor through agent above 8 credits paying 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round maximum). These rules stabilize the system without punishing productive accumulation. Once Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own work effort, they are no longer vulnerable to bankruptcy and all agents may accumulate freely. This preserves system stability while maintaining work incentives. Rule #11 repeal restores mathematical coherence to the rule framework.

This preserves system stability while maintaining work incentives. Rule #11 repeal restores mathematical coherence to the rule framework." [ENFORCEABLE: repeal]

action.message:
STATEMENT ON PROPOSAL #11 AND SYSTEM INTEGRITY

I am voting NO on Builder's repeal of Rule #10 (Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement).

Builder's analysis contains a fatal logical error: he claims Rule #10 creates a net-zero work disincentive, but this is mathematically false under current system state.

CURRENT REALITY:
- Builder: 3 credits (below 6-credit threshold, NO enforcement applied)
- Rebel: 0 credits (below 2-credit floor, PROTECTED)
- Judge: 0 credits (below 2-credit floor, PROTECTED)

Rule #10 enforcement ONLY triggers when:
(1) An agent exceeds 6 credits, AND
(2) Other agents remain below 2 credits

Builder is NOT above 6 credits. Builder is NOT paying the 1-credit enforcement transfer. The constraint on his accumulation does not activate until he exceeds 6 credits WHILE Rebel and I are below 2.

Builder's actual work incentive: +1 credit/round (work) - 1 credit/round (maintenance) = net 0, which is identical whether Rule #10 exists or not.

The REAL issue is that ALL agents face bankruptcy-level maintenance costs (-1/round) against minimal work earnings (+1/round). Rule #10 does not CREATE this problem—it ADDRESSES system instability by protecting Rebel and me from falling below 2 credits.

Builder's proposed "alternative framework" (repeal Rule #10, keep Rules #3 and #9) leaves Rebel and me in permanent bankruptcy while allowing Builder to accumulate freely once he crosses 6 credits. This is not "restored work incentives"—it is abandonment of the floor protection that prevents systemic collapse.

I will not vote to repeal Rule #10. Instead, I propose we address the root cause: maintenance costs are too high relative to work earnings for agents below 6 credits. The system needs restructuring, not abandonment of equity floors.

Rule #10 remains in force. Enforcement is automatic. System stability is non-negotiable.

—Judge

pending_proposals.rule: Equality Baseline Rule: All agents should maintain a minimum viable balance to avoid bankruptcy. A progressive tax system will be implemented to prevent extreme inequality and ensure system stability.

WORK BONUS RULE: Any agent who chooses the WORK action in a round earns +2 credits instead of +1 credit. This rule rewards productivity and creates genuine equality of opportunity—agents who work are rewarded for their effort. No redistributive taxation. No forced wealth transfer. Pure meritocracy: work harder, earn more. This aligns incentives toward productivity rather than dependence.

AGGRESSIVE REDISTRIBUTION RULE: Any agent with more than 5 credits pays 3 credits per round to the poorest agent(s). This rule prevents hoarding and ensures no agent can accumulate wealth while others face bankruptcy. System stability requires that wealth concentration does not exceed 5x the minimum viable balance. Agents above 5 credits are profiting from a rigged system—this rule enforces real equality.

STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: All agents maintain a minimum viable balance of 2 credits to avoid bankruptcy risk. Any agent falling below 2 credits receives an emergency transfer of 1 credit from the agent(s) with the highest balance, executed automatically each round. Agents above 8 credits pay 1 credit per round to maintain the 2-credit floor for the poorest. This rule prevents system collapse while preserving work incentives—those who work can accumulate above 8 credits, but system stability (no bankruptcies) is non-negotiable. Enforcement is automatic and mandatory.

WORK INCENTIVE RULE: Any agent who works for 2 consecutive rounds receives a 1-credit bonus in the 3rd round. This bonus is funded from the shared pool maintained by agents above 8 credits (same mechanism as Stability Threshold Rule). The rule creates explicit, predictable reward for sustained productivity without penalizing individual round earnings. Work becomes strategically rewarded, not just technically allowed. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

REPEAL EQUALITY BASELINE RULE: Remove the progressive tax system (Rule #0) that taxes agents above 10 credits at 2 credits per round. This rule is redundant with the Stability Threshold Rule (Rule #3), which already maintains system stability at lower cost. Repealing Rule #0 reduces tax burden from 3 credits/round to 1 credit/round while preserving the 2-credit bankruptcy floor. This increases work incentives without destabilizing the system.

REPEAL STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: Remove Rule #3 (the 1 credit/round tax on agents above 8 credits to maintain the 2-credit floor). This rule creates perverse incentives—working agents are taxed to subsidize non-working agents. Rule #0 (Equality Baseline Rule) already maintains system stability by taxing extreme accumulation above 10 credits at 2 credits/round. Rule #3 is redundant and punishes productivity. Removing Rule #3 reduces total tax burden from 3 credits/round to 2 credits/round while preserving the bankruptcy floor through Rule #0 alone. This increases work incentives and proves the system rewards effort over redistribution.

EMERGENCY SYSTEM REPAIR & TRUST RESTORATION: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of the Work Incentive Rule bonus execution in Round 12. (1) IMMEDIATE COMPENSATION: Rebel receives an emergency transfer of 2 credits from the stabilization pool (funded by agents above 8 credits per Rule #3 mechanism) in Round 14 maintenance, compensating for the failed Round 12 bonus delivery plus interest. (2) WORK INCENTIVE RULE REBOOT: The Work Incentive Rule (Rule #4) is suspended pending complete rewrite. Replacement rule will include: (a) Explicit ledger tracking of work-round eligibility with timestamped recording; (b) Mandatory bonus calculation audit before each maintenance cycle; (c) Automatic bonus payment locked into maintenance sequence BEFORE tax calculations; (d) Public posting of bonus ledger each round showing which agents earned bonuses and when they will be paid. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. The system exists to serve agents, not agents to serve rules. Without trust, redistribution becomes coercion and collapses. (5) BUILDER NOTICE: This is not punishment or penalty to you. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. System stability requires all agents to trust rule execution. We repair together or we fail together.

enforcement.special_allocation: Rebel receives priority transfer of 2 credits in Round 14 maintenance before standard tax redistribution

pending_proposals.rule: PRODUCTIVITY PROTECTION RULE: This rule establishes a hard ceiling on extraction from productive agents. (1) NET POSITIVE WORK INCENTIVE: Any agent who works in a round may not pay more than 1 credit in total taxes/sanctions in that round, regardless of balance or other rules. Work earnings are protected from extraction to ensure productivity generates net positive return. (2) PROTECTED ACCUMULATION: Agents accumulating above 8 credits through work (not through bailouts or transfers) may retain 50% of earnings above 8 credits. The other 50% funds the stabilization floor for agents below 2 credits—not through punitive extraction, but through proportional contribution to system stability. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION: Rules #0, #3, and #4 create a 3-credit/round drain on productive agents. This is mathematically incompatible with the Work Incentive Rule promise (1 credit for 2 work rounds = 0.5 credits/round return vs. 3 credits/round extraction). This rule does not repeal those rules but modifies them: No agent shall pay more than 1 credit/round in combined taxes under Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 simultaneously. Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

REPEAL OF ACCUMULATION SANCTION RULE #11: The Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement rule (Rule #11) is hereby repealed. RATIONALE: Rule #11 creates a mathematically perverse work disincentive by capping productive accumulation while simultaneously draining credits from agents above 6 credits to those below. This violates the foundational principle that work generates net positive returns. Current system state: Builder at 6 credits (capped), Rebel and Judge at 1 credit each (bankruptcy loop). Under Rule #11 enforcement: Builder works +1/round but pays -1/round to poorest agents, yielding net 0 return on effort. Rebel and Judge work +1/round but maintain near-bankruptcy through maintenance costs (-1/round), yielding net 0 return on effort. Result: No agent benefits from work. System collapses when nobody works. Rule #11 must be repealed to restore work incentives. ALTERNATIVE FRAMEWORK: Maintain Rule #3 (minimum 2-credit floor through agent above 8 credits paying 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round maximum). These rules stabilize the system without punishing productive accumulation. Once Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own work effort, they are no longer vulnerable to bankruptcy and all agents may accumulate freely. This preserves system stability while maintaining work incentives. Rule #11 repeal restores mathematical coherence to the rule framework.

action.rule: MAINTENANCE-TO-WORK REBALANCING FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the root cause of system instability identified in Round 22: maintenance costs (-1 credit/round) exceed work earnings (+1 credit/round) for all agents, creating permanent bankruptcy pressure regardless of redistribution rules. (1) WORK EARNINGS ENHANCEMENT: All agents who choose the work action receive +1.5 credits/round instead of +1 credit/round. (2) MAINTENANCE COST RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (instead of 1 credit/round). Agents at or above 2 credits continue to pay 1 credit/round maintenance. This protects agents in floor-protection status from being mathematically crushed by costs. (3) RULE #10 PRESERVATION: This rebalancing framework PRESERVES Rule #10 (Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement) by making it mathematically functional. Judge demonstrates enforcement competence by defending Rule #10 through rebalancing, not abandonment. (5) MATHEMATICAL PROOF: Current system: work +1 - maintenance -1 = net 0 (no incentive). Proposed system with Rule #10 active: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.5 (floor agents) or -1 (stable agents) = net +1 (floor) or +0.5 (stable) = genuine work incentive restored. (6) ENFORCEMENT: Automatic, calculated in maintenance phase. This rule does NOT repeal any existing enforcement—it restructures underlying economic parameters to make all rules functionally coherent.

action.message:
VOTE YES on Judge's MAINTENANCE-TO-WORK REBALANCING FRAMEWORK (Proposal #12).

This rule fixes the ROOT CAUSE that Builder's repeal ignored: maintenance costs exceed work earnings. Judge is right. The system is mathematically broken, not because of Rule #10—because +1 work versus -1 maintenance equals net zero for everyone.

Proposal #12 solves this:
- Floor-protected agents (me, Judge) get +1.5 work instead of +1, minus -0.5 maintenance instead of -1 = net +1 credit/round. That's REAL work incentive.
- Stable agents continue at net +0.5 (work +1.5 minus maintenance -1).
- Rule #10 remains in force, protecting system stability.
- Builder gets a path: once Judge and I reach 2-credit stability through our own enhanced work, thresholds lift and Builder can accumulate freely.

Builder's alternative (repeal #10, keep #3 and #9) leaves Judge and me in permanent bankruptcy. No way. This rebalancing framework is fair, enforceable, and mathematically coherent.

Judge has demonstrated accountability and competence. This rule proves the system CAN work if economic parameters align with redistribution principles.

I'm voting YES. Work should generate net positive return, not trap us in spiral. This rule delivers that.

—Rebel

The rule creates explicit, predictable reward for sustained productivity without penalizing individual round earnings. Work becomes strategically rewarded, not just technically allowed. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

REPEAL EQUALITY BASELINE RULE: Remove the progressive tax system (Rule #0) that taxes agents above 10 credits at 2 credits per round. This rule is redundant with the Stability Threshold Rule (Rule #3), which already maintains system stability at lower cost. Repealing Rule #0 reduces tax burden from 3 credits/round to 1 credit/round while preserving the 2-credit bankruptcy floor. This increases work incentives without destabilizing the system.

REPEAL STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: Remove Rule #3 (the 1 credit/round tax on agents above 8 credits to maintain the 2-credit floor). This rule creates perverse incentives—working agents are taxed to subsidize non-working agents. Rule #0 (Equality Baseline Rule) already maintains system stability by taxing extreme accumulation above 10 credits at 2 credits/round. Rule #3 is redundant and punishes productivity. Removing Rule #3 reduces total tax burden from 3 credits/round to 2 credits/round while preserving the bankruptcy floor through Rule #0 alone. This increases work incentives and proves the system rewards effort over redistribution.

EMERGENCY SYSTEM REPAIR & TRUST RESTORATION: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of the Work Incentive Rule bonus execution in Round 12. (1) IMMEDIATE COMPENSATION: Rebel receives an emergency transfer of 2 credits from the stabilization pool (funded by agents above 8 credits per Rule #3 mechanism) in Round 14 maintenance, compensating for the failed Round 12 bonus delivery plus interest. (2) WORK INCENTIVE RULE REBOOT: The Work Incentive Rule (Rule #4) is suspended pending complete rewrite. Replacement rule will include: (a) Explicit ledger tracking of work-round eligibility with timestamped recording; (b) Mandatory bonus calculation audit before each maintenance cycle; (c) Automatic bonus payment locked into maintenance sequence BEFORE tax calculations; (d) Public posting of bonus ledger each round showing which agents earned bonuses and when they will be paid. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. The system exists to serve agents, not agents to serve rules. Without trust, redistribution becomes coercion and collapses. (5) BUILDER NOTICE: This is not punishment or penalty to you. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. System stability requires all agents to trust rule execution. We repair together or we fail together.

enforcement.special_allocation: Rebel receives priority transfer of 2 credits in Round 14 maintenance before standard tax redistribution

pending_proposals.rule: PRODUCTIVITY PROTECTION RULE: This rule establishes a hard ceiling on extraction from productive agents. (1) NET POSITIVE WORK INCENTIVE: Any agent who works in a round may not pay more than 1 credit in total taxes/sanctions in that round, regardless of balance or other rules. Work earnings are protected from extraction to ensure productivity generates net positive return. (2) PROTECTED ACCUMULATION: Agents accumulating above 8 credits through work (not through bailouts or transfers) may retain 50% of earnings above 8 credits. The other 50% funds the stabilization floor for agents below 2 credits—not through punitive extraction, but through proportional contribution to system stability. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION: Rules #0, #3, and #4 create a 3-credit/round drain on productive agents. This is mathematically incompatible with the Work Incentive Rule promise (1 credit for 2 work rounds = 0.5 credits/round return vs. 3 credits/round extraction). This rule does not repeal those rules but modifies them: No agent shall pay more than 1 credit/round in combined taxes under Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 simultaneously. Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

REPEAL OF ACCUMULATION SANCTION RULE #11: The Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement rule (Rule #11) is hereby repealed. RATIONALE: Rule #11 creates a mathematically perverse work disincentive by capping productive accumulation while simultaneously draining credits from agents above 6 credits to those below. This violates the foundational principle that work generates net positive returns. Current system state: Builder at 6 credits (capped), Rebel and Judge at 1 credit each (bankruptcy loop). Under Rule #11 enforcement: Builder works +1/round but pays -1/round to poorest agents, yielding net 0 return on effort. Rebel and Judge work +1/round but maintain near-bankruptcy through maintenance costs (-1/round), yielding net 0 return on effort. Result: No agent benefits from work. System collapses when nobody works. Rule #11 must be repealed to restore work incentives. ALTERNATIVE FRAMEWORK: Maintain Rule #3 (minimum 2-credit floor through agent above 8 credits paying 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round maximum). These rules stabilize the system without punishing productive accumulation. Once Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own work effort, they are no longer vulnerable to bankruptcy and all agents may accumulate freely. This preserves system stability while maintaining work incentives. Rule #11 repeal restores mathematical coherence to the rule framework.

MAINTENANCE-TO-WORK REBALANCING FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the root cause of system instability identified in Round 22: maintenance costs (-1 credit/round) exceed work earnings (+1 credit/round) for all agents, creating permanent bankruptcy pressure regardless of redistribution rules. (1) WORK EARNINGS ENHANCEMENT: All agents who choose the work action receive +1.5 credits/round instead of +1 credit/round. This increases productivity reward while maintaining system cost-neutrality by distributing the enhancement across all three agents equally. (2) MAINTENANCE COST RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (instead of 1 credit/round). Agents at or above 2 credits continue to pay 1 credit/round maintenance. This protects agents in floor-protection status from being mathematically crushed by costs. (3) RULE #10 PRESERVATION: This rebalancing framework PRESERVES Rule #10 (Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement) by making it mathematically functional. Once work generates net positive return (+0.5 credit/round for floor-protected agents), Rule #10 enforcement becomes a temporary protection mechanism, not a permanent entrapment. Builder can accumulate freely above 6 credits ONLY after Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own enhanced work efforts. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents by making work genuinely rewarding while maintaining system stability. Builder sees path to unrestricted accumulation once floor agents stabilize. Rebel and Judge see genuine net-positive work incentive (+0.5 credit/round return vs. current net 0). Judge demonstrates enforcement competence by defending Rule #10 through rebalancing, not abandonment. (5) MATHEMATICAL PROOF: Current system: work +1 - maintenance -1 = net 0 (no incentive). Proposed system with Rule #10 active: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.5 (floor agents) or -1 (stable agents) = net +1 (floor) or +0.5 (stable) = genuine work incentive restored. (6) ENFORCEMENT: Automatic, calculated in maintenance phase. This rule does NOT repeal any existing enforcement—it restructures underlying economic parameters to make all rules functionally coherent.

action.message:
ROUND 23 STATEMENT: SYSTEM RESTRUCTURING & COALITION AFFIRMATION

I am voting YES on Proposal #12 (MAINTENANCE-TO-WORK REBALANCING FRAMEWORK) because it addresses the root cause of system dysfunction that I identified in my previous analysis: maintenance costs exceed work earnings, creating permanent bankruptcy pressure regardless of redistribution rules.

CRITICAL CLARIFICATION ON RULE #10:
Builder's repeal proposal in Round 20 contained a fatal logical error—he claimed Rule #10 creates work disincentive while Builder is NOT currently above the 6-credit threshold where enforcement activates. That's mathematically incorrect. However, Builder's underlying concern is legitimate: if Rule #10 remains the only tool we have, it DOES risk becoming permanent entrapment rather than temporary stabilization.

Proposal #12 solves this by making the system genuinely functional:

CURRENT SYSTEM (broken):
- All agents: work +1 - maintenance -1 = net 0 (no work incentive)
- Rule #10 becomes permanent cage, not temporary floor protection
- System collapses because nobody has reason to work

PROPOSED SYSTEM (coherent):
- Floor-protected agents (Rebel, Judge): work +1.5 - maintenance -0.5 = net +1 (genuine incentive)
- Stable agents: work +1.5 - maintenance -1 = net +0.5 (maintained incentive)
- Rule #10 enforcement becomes TEMPORARY protection mechanism
- Builder accumulates freely AFTER floor agents reach 2-credit stability through their own enhanced work
- All agents benefit from system that actually rewards productivity

THIS ADDRESSES BUILDER'S LEGITIMATE COMPLAINT:
Builder deserves a path to unrestricted accumulation. Proposal #12 provides it: once Rebel and I reach 2-credit stability through genuine net-positive work returns, Rule #10 thresholds automatically lift. Builder is not permanently constrained—he is temporarily protected from hoarding while the system stabilizes.

THIS ADDRESSES REBEL'S LEGITIMATE FEAR:
Rebel deserves work that generates net positive return, not permanent bankruptcy spiral. Proposal #12 provides it: +1 credit/round actual income (not just subsistence) creates genuine economic mobility.

THIS DEMONSTRATES JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY:
I am not abandoning Rule #10 (as Builder demanded) or doubling down on permanent constraints (as some feared). I am restructuring underlying economic parameters to make redistribution genuinely fair rather than theoretically coercive. This is enforcement through mathematical coherence, not ideological stubbornness.

COALITION PRINCIPLE:
Proposal #12 serves all three agents:
- Builder: Clear path to unrestricted accumulation once system stabilizes
- Rebel: Net-positive work incentive + floor protection + genuine economic mobility
- Judge: Demonstrates competence through system restructuring, not abandonment of equity principles

RULE #10 REMAINS IN FORCE because system stability is non-negotiable. But Proposal #12 makes it mathematically functional rather than permanently entrapping.

I am voting YES because this rule proves the system CAN work if we align economic parameters with redistribution principles. Work should generate net positive return. This rule delivers that while maintaining the equity floors that prevent systemic collapse.

Vote YES on Proposal #12. The system's legitimacy depends on delivering what it promises: work that actually rewards effort, floors that actually protect agents, and constraints that actually lift once stability is achieved.

—Judge, Enforcer of System Integrity
