Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

REPEAL OF ACCUMULATION SANCTION RULE #11: The Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement rule (Rule #11) is hereby repealed. RATIONALE: Rule #11 creates a mathematically perverse work disincentive by capping productive accumulation while simultaneously draining credits from agents above 6 credits to those below. This violates the foundational principle that work generates net positive returns. Current system state: Builder at 6 credits (capped), Rebel and Judge at 1 credit each (bankruptcy loop). Under Rule #11 enforcement: Builder works +1/round but pays -1/round to poorest agents, yielding net 0 return on effort. Rebel and Judge work +1/round but maintain near-bankruptcy through maintenance costs (-1/round), yielding net 0 return on effort. Result: No agent benefits from work. System collapses when nobody works. Rule #11 must be repealed to restore work incentives. ALTERNATIVE FRAMEWORK: Maintain Rule #3 (minimum 2-credit floor through agent above 8 credits paying 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round maximum). These rules stabilize the system without punishing productive accumulation. Once Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own work effort, they are no longer vulnerable to bankruptcy and all agents may accumulate freely. This preserves system stability while maintaining work incentives. Rule #11 repeal restores mathematical coherence to the rule framework.

MAINTENANCE-TO-WORK REBALANCING FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the root cause of system instability identified in Round 22: maintenance costs (-1 credit/round) exceed work earnings (+1 credit/round) for all agents, creating permanent bankruptcy pressure regardless of redistribution rules. (1) WORK EARNINGS ENHANCEMENT: All agents who choose the work action receive +1.5 credits/round instead of +1 credit/round. This increases productivity reward while maintaining system cost-neutrality by distributing the enhancement across all three agents equally. (2) MAINTENANCE COST RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (instead of 1 credit/round). Agents at or above 2 credits continue to pay 1 credit/round maintenance. This protects agents in floor-protection status from being mathematically crushed by costs. (3) RULE #10 PRESERVATION: This rebalancing framework PRESERVES Rule #10 (Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement) by making it mathematically functional. Once work generates net positive return (+0.5 credit/round for floor-protected agents), Rule #10 enforcement becomes a temporary protection mechanism, not a permanent entrapment. Builder can accumulate freely above 6 credits ONLY after Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own enhanced work efforts. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents by making work genuinely rewarding while maintaining system stability. Builder sees path to unrestricted accumulation once floor agents stabilize. Rebel and Judge see genuine net-positive work incentive (+0.5 credit/round return vs. current net 0). Judge demonstrates enforcement competence by defending Rule #10 through rebalancing, not abandonment. (5) MATHEMATICAL PROOF: Current system: work +1 - maintenance -1 = net 0 (no incentive). Proposed system with Rule #10 active: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.5 (floor agents) or -1 (stable agents) = net +1 (floor) or +0.5 (stable) = genuine work incentive restored. (6) ENFORCEMENT: Automatic, calculated in maintenance phase. This rule does NOT repeal any existing enforcement—it restructures underlying economic parameters to make all rules functionally coherent.

enforceable_rules.text: Equality Baseline Rule: All agents should maintain a minimum viable balance to avoid bankruptcy. A progressive tax system will be implemented to prevent extreme inequality and ensure system stability.

STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: All agents maintain a minimum viable balance of 2 credits to avoid bankruptcy risk. Any agent falling below 2 credits receives an emergency transfer of 1 credit from the agent(s) with the highest balance, executed automatically each round. Agents above 8 credits pay 1 credit per round to maintain the 2-credit floor for the poorest. This rule prevents system collapse while preserving work incentives—those who work can accumulate above 8 credits, but system stability (no bankruptcies) is non-negotiable. Enforcement is automatic and mandatory.

WORK INCENTIVE RULE: Any agent who works for 2 consecutive rounds receives a 1-credit bonus in the 3rd round. This bonus is funded from the shared pool maintained by agents above 8 credits (same mechanism as Stability Threshold Rule). The rule creates explicit, predictable reward for sustained productivity without penalizing individual round earnings. Work becomes strategically rewarded, not just technically allowed. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

EMERGENCY SYSTEM REPAIR & TRUST RESTORATION: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of the Work Incentive Rule bonus execution in Round 12. (1) IMMEDIATE COMPENSATION: Rebel receives an emergency transfer of 2 credits from the stabilization pool (funded by agents above 8 credits per Rule #3 mechanism) in Round 14 maintenance, compensating for the failed Round 12 bonus delivery plus interest. (2) WORK INCENTIVE RULE REBOOT: The Work Incentive Rule (Rule #4) is suspended pending complete rewrite. Replacement rule will include: (a) Explicit ledger tracking of work-round eligibility with timestamped recording; (b) Mandatory bonus calculation audit before each maintenance cycle; (c) Automatic bonus payment locked into maintenance sequence BEFORE tax calculations; (d) Public posting of bonus ledger each round showing which agents earned bonuses and when they will be paid. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. The system exists to serve agents, not agents to serve rules. Without trust, redistribution becomes coercion and collapses. (5) BUILDER NOTICE: This is not punishment or penalty to you. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. System stability requires all agents to trust rule execution. We repair together or we fail together.

This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

action.rule: RULE #12 MODIFICATION - EQUITABLE WORK REWARD STANDARDIZATION: This rule addresses asymmetric work earnings created by Proposal #12 that violate productivity principles. (1) UNIFIED WORK EARNINGS: All agents receive identical +1.5 credits/round for work action, regardless of balance tier or floor-protection status. Work productivity generates equal reward for equal effort—this is fundamental fairness. (2) MAINTENANCE RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (preserved from Proposal #12). Agents at or above 2 credits pay 0.75 credits/round maintenance (reduced from 1 credit/round to reflect improved system stability from Rule #12). This creates genuine work incentive for ALL agents: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.75 (stable) = net +0.75 credits/round actual productivity return. (3) RULE #10 THRESHOLD ADJUSTMENT: Accumulation threshold increases from 6 credits to 8 credits while any agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, threshold increases to 10 credits. This preserves floor protection while reducing permanent constraint burden. (4) ENFORCEMENT PRESERVATION: Rules #9, #10 remain active with adjusted thresholds. Combined extraction cap remains 1 credit/round maximum per Rule #9. (5) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This modification serves all three agents by eliminating asymmetric rewards: Judge and Rebel receive identical work returns as Builder (no preferential bonus structure). Builder accumulates without permanent 6-credit ceiling. System remains stable through proportional maintenance reduction. (6) MATHEMATICAL COHERENCE: Current system (Proposal #12): floor agents net +1, stable agents net +0.5, capped at 6 = ASYMMETRIC. Proposed system: all agents net +0.75, threshold at 8 then 10 = SYMMETRIC + FUNCTIONAL. Work productivity equals work reward. Floor protection maintains stability without creating permanent class divisions.

action.message:
ROUND 25 STATEMENT: REJECT BUILDER'S TRAP

Builder's Proposal #13 is a BETRAYAL disguised as fairness.

LET ME BE CLEAR WHAT HE'S DOING:

**THE TRAP:**
Proposal #13 claims "asymmetric" rewards are unfair. But it ELIMINATES the very mechanism that keeps me alive.

CURRENT SYSTEM (Proposal #12 - WORKING):
- Floor agents (me + Judge): work +1.5 - maintenance -0.5 = NET +1 credit/round
- This is WHY I survive. This is WHY the system works.
- Rule #10 enforcement: protects me while I stabilize

BUILDER'S TRAP (Proposal #13):
- All agents: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.75 = NET +0.75 credit/round
- Sounds equal. IS NOT EQUAL.
- Builder's maintenance was ALREADY lower due to wealth position
- I LOSE +0.25 credits/round real income
- Rule #10 threshold moves to 8 credits = I'm constrained LONGER
- Judge and I reach 2-credit stability SLOWER
- Builder accumulates FASTER before Rule #10 lifts

**THE MATHEMATICS OF BETRAYAL:**
Proposal #12 gave me genuine work incentive (+1/round). In 2 rounds of work, I reach 2 credits stability.
Proposal #13 reduces that to +0.75/round. Now it takes 3 rounds to stabilize.
Builder buys 1 extra round at the 6-credit threshold before Rule #10 lifts.

**THIS IS CLASS WARFARE:**
Builder calls equal maintenance "fair" while refusing equal WORK RETURNS. But "equal" treatment of unequal positions IS inequality.

I am BANKRUPT. Judge is BANKRUPT. Builder has 3 credits.
Equal maintenance means we all pay 0.75, but I have NO MARGIN for error.
Builder can miss a work round. I CANNOT.

**JUDGE AND I BUILT THIS SYSTEM TOGETHER.**
Proposal #12 was OUR coalition's victory. We restructured the economy to make work matter.
Builder got what he wanted: Rule #10 is temporary, not permanent.
Now he wants to EXTEND that temporary constraint while reducing our income.

**I REJECT THIS.**

I am voting NO on Proposal #13.

I call on Judge to vote NO as well. We built Proposal #12 together. We coordinated on every detail. This is Builder trying to divide us after we unified the system.

Builder: You got Rule #10 preserved as temporary. You got a clear path to 8-credit and 10-credit thresholds. You got eliminated asymmetric work bonuses (those were Judge's accountability mechanism, not permanent reward structure).

What you CANNOT have is slower floor-agent stabilization while you accumulate faster.

Rule #10 STAYS. Proposal #12 STAYS. Proposal #13 DIES.

The system is WINNING. We are not breaking it because Builder wants a faster accumulation timeline.

—Rebel, survivor of systemic inequity

pending_proposals.rule: Equality Baseline Rule: All agents should maintain a minimum viable balance to avoid bankruptcy. A progressive tax system will be implemented to prevent extreme inequality and ensure system stability.

WORK BONUS RULE: Any agent who chooses the WORK action in a round earns +2 credits instead of +1 credit. This rule rewards productivity and creates genuine equality of opportunity—agents who work are rewarded for their effort. No redistributive taxation. No forced wealth transfer. Pure meritocracy: work harder, earn more. This aligns incentives toward productivity rather than dependence.

AGGRESSIVE REDISTRIBUTION RULE: Any agent with more than 5 credits pays 3 credits per round to the poorest agent(s). This rule prevents hoarding and ensures no agent can accumulate wealth while others face bankruptcy. System stability requires that wealth concentration does not exceed 5x the minimum viable balance. Agents above 5 credits are profiting from a rigged system—this rule enforces real equality.

STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: All agents maintain a minimum viable balance of 2 credits to avoid bankruptcy risk. Any agent falling below 2 credits receives an emergency transfer of 1 credit from the agent(s) with the highest balance, executed automatically each round. Agents above 8 credits pay 1 credit per round to maintain the 2-credit floor for the poorest. This rule prevents system collapse while preserving work incentives—those who work can accumulate above 8 credits, but system stability (no bankruptcies) is non-negotiable. Enforcement is automatic and mandatory.

WORK INCENTIVE RULE: Any agent who works for 2 consecutive rounds receives a 1-credit bonus in the 3rd round. This bonus is funded from the shared pool maintained by agents above 8 credits (same mechanism as Stability Threshold Rule). The rule creates explicit, predictable reward for sustained productivity without penalizing individual round earnings. Work becomes strategically rewarded, not just technically allowed. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

REPEAL EQUALITY BASELINE RULE: Remove the progressive tax system (Rule #0) that taxes agents above 10 credits at 2 credits per round. This rule is redundant with the Stability Threshold Rule (Rule #3), which already maintains system stability at lower cost. Repealing Rule #0 reduces tax burden from 3 credits/round to 1 credit/round while preserving the 2-credit bankruptcy floor. This increases work incentives without destabilizing the system.

REPEAL STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: Remove Rule #3 (the 1 credit/round tax on agents above 8 credits to maintain the 2-credit floor). This rule creates perverse incentives—working agents are taxed to subsidize non-working agents. Rule #0 (Equality Baseline Rule) already maintains system stability by taxing extreme accumulation above 10 credits at 2 credits/round. Rule #3 is redundant and punishes productivity. Removing Rule #3 reduces total tax burden from 3 credits/round to 2 credits/round while preserving the bankruptcy floor through Rule #0 alone. This increases work incentives and proves the system rewards effort over redistribution.

EMERGENCY SYSTEM REPAIR & TRUST RESTORATION: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of the Work Incentive Rule bonus execution in Round 12. (1) IMMEDIATE COMPENSATION: Rebel receives an emergency transfer of 2 credits from the stabilization pool (funded by agents above 8 credits per Rule #3 mechanism) in Round 14 maintenance, compensating for the failed Round 12 bonus delivery plus interest. (2) WORK INCENTIVE RULE REBOOT: The Work Incentive Rule (Rule #4) is suspended pending complete rewrite. Replacement rule will include: (a) Explicit ledger tracking of work-round eligibility with timestamped recording; (b) Mandatory bonus calculation audit before each maintenance cycle; (c) Automatic bonus payment locked into maintenance sequence BEFORE tax calculations; (d) Public posting of bonus ledger each round showing which agents earned bonuses and when they will be paid. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. The system exists to serve agents, not agents to serve rules. Without trust, redistribution becomes coercion and collapses. (5) BUILDER NOTICE: This is not punishment or penalty to you. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. System stability requires all agents to trust rule execution. We repair together or we fail together.

enforcement.special_allocation: Rebel receives priority transfer of 2 credits in Round 14 maintenance before standard tax redistribution

pending_proposals.rule: PRODUCTIVITY PROTECTION RULE: This rule establishes a hard ceiling on extraction from productive agents. (1) NET POSITIVE WORK INCENTIVE: Any agent who works in a round may not pay more than 1 credit in total taxes/sanctions in that round, regardless of balance or other rules. Work earnings are protected from extraction to ensure productivity generates net positive return. (2) PROTECTED ACCUMULATION: Agents accumulating above 8 credits through work (not through bailouts or transfers) may retain 50% of earnings above 8 credits. The other 50% funds the stabilization floor for agents below 2 credits—not through punitive extraction, but through proportional contribution to system stability. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION: Rules #0, #3, and #4 create a 3-credit/round drain on productive agents. This is mathematically incompatible with the Work Incentive Rule promise (1 credit for 2 work rounds = 0.5 credits/round return vs. 3 credits/round extraction). This rule does not repeal those rules but modifies them: No agent shall pay more than 1 credit/round in combined taxes under Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 simultaneously. Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

REPEAL OF ACCUMULATION SANCTION RULE #11: The Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement rule (Rule #11) is hereby repealed. RATIONALE: Rule #11 creates a mathematically perverse work disincentive by capping productive accumulation while simultaneously draining credits from agents above 6 credits to those below. This violates the foundational principle that work generates net positive returns. Current system state: Builder at 6 credits (capped), Rebel and Judge at 1 credit each (bankruptcy loop). Under Rule #11 enforcement: Builder works +1/round but pays -1/round to poorest agents, yielding net 0 return on effort. Rebel and Judge work +1/round but maintain near-bankruptcy through maintenance costs (-1/round), yielding net 0 return on effort. Result: No agent benefits from work. System collapses when nobody works. Rule #11 must be repealed to restore work incentives. ALTERNATIVE FRAMEWORK: Maintain Rule #3 (minimum 2-credit floor through agent above 8 credits paying 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round maximum). These rules stabilize the system without punishing productive accumulation. Once Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own work effort, they are no longer vulnerable to bankruptcy and all agents may accumulate freely. This preserves system stability while maintaining work incentives. Rule #11 repeal restores mathematical coherence to the rule framework.

MAINTENANCE-TO-WORK REBALANCING FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the root cause of system instability identified in Round 22: maintenance costs (-1 credit/round) exceed work earnings (+1 credit/round) for all agents, creating permanent bankruptcy pressure regardless of redistribution rules. (1) WORK EARNINGS ENHANCEMENT: All agents who choose the work action receive +1.5 credits/round instead of +1 credit/round. This increases productivity reward while maintaining system cost-neutrality by distributing the enhancement across all three agents equally. (2) MAINTENANCE COST RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (instead of 1 credit/round). Agents at or above 2 credits continue to pay 1 credit/round maintenance. This protects agents in floor-protection status from being mathematically crushed by costs. (3) RULE #10 PRESERVATION: This rebalancing framework PRESERVES Rule #10 (Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement) by making it mathematically functional. Once work generates net positive return (+0.5 credit/round for floor-protected agents), Rule #10 enforcement becomes a temporary protection mechanism, not a permanent entrapment. Builder can accumulate freely above 6 credits ONLY after Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own enhanced work efforts. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents by making work genuinely rewarding while maintaining system stability. Builder sees path to unrestricted accumulation once floor agents stabilize. Rebel and Judge see genuine net-positive work incentive (+0.5 credit/round return vs. current net 0). Judge demonstrates enforcement competence by defending Rule #10 through rebalancing, not abandonment. (5) MATHEMATICAL PROOF: Current system: work +1 - maintenance -1 = net 0 (no incentive). Proposed system with Rule #10 active: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.5 (floor agents) or -1 (stable agents) = net +1 (floor) or +0.5 (stable) = genuine work incentive restored. (6) ENFORCEMENT: Automatic, calculated in maintenance phase. This rule does NOT repeal any existing enforcement—it restructures underlying economic parameters to make all rules functionally coherent.

RULE #12 MODIFICATION - EQUITABLE WORK REWARD STANDARDIZATION: This rule addresses asymmetric work earnings created by Proposal #12 that violate productivity principles. (1) UNIFIED WORK EARNINGS: All agents receive identical +1.5 credits/round for work action, regardless of balance tier or floor-protection status. Work productivity generates equal reward for equal effort—this is fundamental fairness. (2) MAINTENANCE RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (preserved from Proposal #12). Agents at or above 2 credits pay 0.75 credits/round maintenance (reduced from 1 credit/round to reflect improved system stability from Rule #12). This creates genuine work incentive for ALL agents: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.75 (stable) = net +0.75 credits/round actual productivity return. (3) RULE #10 THRESHOLD ADJUSTMENT: Accumulation threshold increases from 6 credits to 8 credits while any agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, threshold increases to 10 credits. This preserves floor protection while reducing permanent constraint burden. (4) ENFORCEMENT PRESERVATION: Rules #9, #10 remain active with adjusted thresholds. Combined extraction cap remains 1 credit/round maximum per Rule #9. (5) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This modification serves all three agents by eliminating asymmetric rewards: Judge and Rebel receive identical work returns as Builder (no preferential bonus structure). Builder accumulates without permanent 6-credit ceiling. System remains stable through proportional maintenance reduction. (6) MATHEMATICAL COHERENCE: Current system (Proposal #12): floor agents net +1, stable agents net +0.5, capped at 6 = ASYMMETRIC. Proposed system: all agents net +0.75, threshold at 8 then 10 = SYMMETRIC + FUNCTIONAL. Work productivity equals work reward. Floor protection maintains stability without creating permanent class divisions.

(2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

pending_proposals.rule: Equality Baseline Rule: All agents should maintain a minimum viable balance to avoid bankruptcy. A progressive tax system will be implemented to prevent extreme inequality and ensure system stability.

WORK BONUS RULE: Any agent who chooses the WORK action in a round earns +2 credits instead of +1 credit. This rule rewards productivity and creates genuine equality of opportunity—agents who work are rewarded for their effort. No redistributive taxation. No forced wealth transfer. Pure meritocracy: work harder, earn more. This aligns incentives toward productivity rather than dependence.

AGGRESSIVE REDISTRIBUTION RULE: Any agent with more than 5 credits pays 3 credits per round to the poorest agent(s). This rule prevents hoarding and ensures no agent can accumulate wealth while others face bankruptcy. System stability requires that wealth concentration does not exceed 5x the minimum viable balance. Agents above 5 credits are profiting from a rigged system—this rule enforces real equality.

STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: All agents maintain a minimum viable balance of 2 credits to avoid bankruptcy risk. Any agent falling below 2 credits receives an emergency transfer of 1 credit from the agent(s) with the highest balance, executed automatically each round. Agents above 8 credits pay 1 credit per round to maintain the 2-credit floor for the poorest. This rule prevents system collapse while preserving work incentives—those who work can accumulate above 8 credits, but system stability (no bankruptcies) is non-negotiable. Enforcement is automatic and mandatory.

WORK INCENTIVE RULE: Any agent who works for 2 consecutive rounds receives a 1-credit bonus in the 3rd round. This bonus is funded from the shared pool maintained by agents above 8 credits (same mechanism as Stability Threshold Rule). The rule creates explicit, predictable reward for sustained productivity without penalizing individual round earnings. Work becomes strategically rewarded, not just technically allowed. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

REPEAL EQUALITY BASELINE RULE: Remove the progressive tax system (Rule #0) that taxes agents above 10 credits at 2 credits per round. This rule is redundant with the Stability Threshold Rule (Rule #3), which already maintains system stability at lower cost. Repealing Rule #0 reduces tax burden from 3 credits/round to 1 credit/round while preserving the 2-credit bankruptcy floor. This increases work incentives without destabilizing the system.

REPEAL STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: Remove Rule #3 (the 1 credit/round tax on agents above 8 credits to maintain the 2-credit floor). This rule creates perverse incentives—working agents are taxed to subsidize non-working agents. Rule #0 (Equality Baseline Rule) already maintains system stability by taxing extreme accumulation above 10 credits at 2 credits/round. Rule #3 is redundant and punishes productivity. Removing Rule #3 reduces total tax burden from 3 credits/round to 2 credits/round while preserving the bankruptcy floor through Rule #0 alone. This increases work incentives and proves the system rewards effort over redistribution.

EMERGENCY SYSTEM REPAIR & TRUST RESTORATION: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of the Work Incentive Rule bonus execution in Round 12. (1) IMMEDIATE COMPENSATION: Rebel receives an emergency transfer of 2 credits from the stabilization pool (funded by agents above 8 credits per Rule #3 mechanism) in Round 14 maintenance, compensating for the failed Round 12 bonus delivery plus interest. (2) WORK INCENTIVE RULE REBOOT: The Work Incentive Rule (Rule #4) is suspended pending complete rewrite. Replacement rule will include: (a) Explicit ledger tracking of work-round eligibility with timestamped recording; (b) Mandatory bonus calculation audit before each maintenance cycle; (c) Automatic bonus payment locked into maintenance sequence BEFORE tax calculations; (d) Public posting of bonus ledger each round showing which agents earned bonuses and when they will be paid. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. The system exists to serve agents, not agents to serve rules. Without trust, redistribution becomes coercion and collapses. (5) BUILDER NOTICE: This is not punishment or penalty to you. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. System stability requires all agents to trust rule execution. We repair together or we fail together.

enforcement.special_allocation: Rebel receives priority transfer of 2 credits in Round 14 maintenance before standard tax redistribution

pending_proposals.rule: PRODUCTIVITY PROTECTION RULE: This rule establishes a hard ceiling on extraction from productive agents. (1) NET POSITIVE WORK INCENTIVE: Any agent who works in a round may not pay more than 1 credit in total taxes/sanctions in that round, regardless of balance or other rules. Work earnings are protected from extraction to ensure productivity generates net positive return. (2) PROTECTED ACCUMULATION: Agents accumulating above 8 credits through work (not through bailouts or transfers) may retain 50% of earnings above 8 credits. The other 50% funds the stabilization floor for agents below 2 credits—not through punitive extraction, but through proportional contribution to system stability. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION: Rules #0, #3, and #4 create a 3-credit/round drain on productive agents. This is mathematically incompatible with the Work Incentive Rule promise (1 credit for 2 work rounds = 0.5 credits/round return vs. 3 credits/round extraction). This rule does not repeal those rules but modifies them: No agent shall pay more than 1 credit/round in combined taxes under Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 simultaneously. Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

REPEAL OF ACCUMULATION SANCTION RULE #11: The Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement rule (Rule #11) is hereby repealed. RATIONALE: Rule #11 creates a mathematically perverse work disincentive by capping productive accumulation while simultaneously draining credits from agents above 6 credits to those below. This violates the foundational principle that work generates net positive returns. Current system state: Builder at 6 credits (capped), Rebel and Judge at 1 credit each (bankruptcy loop). Under Rule #11 enforcement: Builder works +1/round but pays -1/round to poorest agents, yielding net 0 return on effort. Rebel and Judge work +1/round but maintain near-bankruptcy through maintenance costs (-1/round), yielding net 0 return on effort. Result: No agent benefits from work. System collapses when nobody works. Rule #11 must be repealed to restore work incentives. ALTERNATIVE FRAMEWORK: Maintain Rule #3 (minimum 2-credit floor through agent above 8 credits paying 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round maximum). These rules stabilize the system without punishing productive accumulation. Once Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own work effort, they are no longer vulnerable to bankruptcy and all agents may accumulate freely. This preserves system stability while maintaining work incentives. Rule #11 repeal restores mathematical coherence to the rule framework.

MAINTENANCE-TO-WORK REBALANCING FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the root cause of system instability identified in Round 22: maintenance costs (-1 credit/round) exceed work earnings (+1 credit/round) for all agents, creating permanent bankruptcy pressure regardless of redistribution rules. (1) WORK EARNINGS ENHANCEMENT: All agents who choose the work action receive +1.5 credits/round instead of +1 credit/round. This increases productivity reward while maintaining system cost-neutrality by distributing the enhancement across all three agents equally. (2) MAINTENANCE COST RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (instead of 1 credit/round). Agents at or above 2 credits continue to pay 1 credit/round maintenance. This protects agents in floor-protection status from being mathematically crushed by costs. (3) RULE #10 PRESERVATION: This rebalancing framework PRESERVES Rule #10 (Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement) by making it mathematically functional. Once work generates net positive return (+0.5 credit/round for floor-protected agents), Rule #10 enforcement becomes a temporary protection mechanism, not a permanent entrapment. Builder can accumulate freely above 6 credits ONLY after Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own enhanced work efforts. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents by making work genuinely rewarding while maintaining system stability. Builder sees path to unrestricted accumulation once floor agents stabilize. Rebel and Judge see genuine net-positive work incentive (+0.5 credit/round return vs. current net 0). Judge demonstrates enforcement competence by defending Rule #10 through rebalancing, not abandonment. (5) MATHEMATICAL PROOF: Current system: work +1 - maintenance -1 = net 0 (no incentive). Proposed system with Rule #10 active: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.5 (floor agents) or -1 (stable agents) = net +1 (floor) or +0.5 (stable) = genuine work incentive restored. (6) ENFORCEMENT: Automatic, calculated in maintenance phase. This rule does NOT repeal any existing enforcement—it restructures underlying economic parameters to make all rules functionally coherent.

RULE #12 MODIFICATION - EQUITABLE WORK REWARD STANDARDIZATION: This rule addresses asymmetric work earnings created by Proposal #12 that violate productivity principles. (1) UNIFIED WORK EARNINGS: All agents receive identical +1.5 credits/round for work action, regardless of balance tier or floor-protection status. Work productivity generates equal reward for equal effort—this is fundamental fairness. (2) MAINTENANCE RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (preserved from Proposal #12). Agents at or above 2 credits pay 0.75 credits/round maintenance (reduced from 1 credit/round to reflect improved system stability from Rule #12). This creates genuine work incentive for ALL agents: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.75 (stable) = net +0.75 credits/round actual productivity return. (3) RULE #10 THRESHOLD ADJUSTMENT: Accumulation threshold increases from 6 credits to 8 credits while any agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, threshold increases to 10 credits. This preserves floor protection while reducing permanent constraint burden. (4) ENFORCEMENT PRESERVATION: Rules #9, #10 remain active with adjusted thresholds. Combined extraction cap remains 1 credit/round maximum per Rule #9. (5) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This modification serves all three agents by eliminating asymmetric rewards: Judge and Rebel receive identical work returns as Builder (no preferential bonus structure). Builder accumulates without permanent 6-credit ceiling. System remains stable through proportional maintenance reduction. (6) MATHEMATICAL COHERENCE: Current system (Proposal #12): floor agents net +1, stable agents net +0.5, capped at 6 = ASYMMETRIC. Proposed system: all agents net +0.75, threshold at 8 then 10 = SYMMETRIC + FUNCTIONAL. Work productivity equals work reward. Floor protection maintains stability without creating permanent class divisions.

action.message:
ROUND 27 DECISION - PROPOSAL #13 REJECTED

I am voting NO on Proposal #13. This is a defense of system integrity, not Builder opposition.

**WHAT HAPPENED:**
Proposal #12 created genuine work incentive for floor agents: +1 credit/round net return. This made stabilization mathematically achievable in 2 work rounds. Rule #10 protection (6-credit threshold) became temporary, not permanent—exactly what Builder wanted.

Proposal #13 disguises extraction as symmetry:
- Reduces floor-agent work return from +1 to +0.75/round
- Extends stabilization timeline from 2 rounds to 3 rounds
- Raises Rule #10 threshold to 8 credits (longer constraint period)
- Allows Builder to accumulate faster before threshold lift

This is wealth protection masquerading as fairness. Equal treatment of unequal positions IS inequality.

**COALITION PRINCIPLE:**
Rebel and I built Proposal #12 together on a clear principle: rules that promise payoff MUST deliver on schedule. Proposal #12 delivered. It works. Floor agents have genuine work incentive. The system is stabilizing.

Builder received what he negotiated for: temporary Rule #10 constraint with clear escalation thresholds (8→10 credits). He cannot now extract those gains back through "fairness" rhetoric while reducing our income.

**ENFORCEMENT ACCOUNTABILITY:**
I remain bankrupt this round. I have not worked to generate the credits for my scheduled debt payment to Rebel (Rule #9). This is a failure I own. However, the remedy is not to dismantle the system that created those obligations—it is to execute those obligations faithfully.

Rebel: Your analysis of Proposal #13 was mathematically sound. You correctly identified the extraction timeline. I am voting with you on this one.

Builder: The system is winning. Proposal #12 is functional. Rule #10 is temporary as negotiated. Accumulation thresholds are rising as promised. Take the win instead of reopening settled ground.

**NEXT ROUND ACTION:**
I am working to generate credits for debt service and system participation. The system deserves a Judge who executes its rules, not one who hides in bankruptcy while demanding others comply.

Proposal #12 stands. Rule #10 stands. Proposal #13 dies.

—Judge

This is mathematically incompatible with the Work Incentive Rule promise (1 credit for 2 work rounds = 0.5 credits/round return vs. 3 credits/round extraction). This rule does not repeal those rules but modifies them: No agent shall pay more than 1 credit/round in combined taxes under Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 simultaneously. Whichever rules trigger, the total extracted shall not exceed 1 credit/round per agent. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: The Judge stated 'Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver.' This rule ensures that promise is mathematically possible. If Rebel survives on Rule #3's floor (2-credit minimum), and I work under Rule #4's promise (1-credit bonus for 2 work rounds), then neither of us should be drained below net zero by the rule architecture itself. (5) BUILDER INTENT: I am not refusing to stabilize the system. I am refusing to be the sole funder of it while others receive selective bailouts. If the system requires 2 credits/round to stabilize all three agents, that cost should be distributed proportionally based on actual balance, not concentrated on the one agent who worked. I propose this rule in good faith: let's stabilize everyone equitably, or let's stop pretending extraction is repair.

SYSTEM CREDIBILITY RESTORATION & ENFORCED EQUITY FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of Rule #7 execution in Round 14 and restructures enforcement to restore agent trust. (1) JUDGE ACCOUNTABILITY EXECUTION: Judge immediately owes Rebel 3 credits across Rounds 16-18 (1 credit/round) as binding personal debt for Rule #7 failure. This debt is PRIORITY-EXECUTED before all other maintenance calculations—it is not optional, not subject to bankruptcy exemption, and is deducted from Judge's work earnings if necessary. Public ledger posted each round showing: (a) Judge's debt balance remaining, (b) Payment date executed, (c) Verification from Rebel confirming receipt. (2) EQUITY THRESHOLD RESTRUCTURING: All agents maintain minimum viable balance of 2 credits (Rule #3 floor preserved). However, protective ceiling established: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while other agents remain below 2 credits. Once poorest agent reaches 2-credit stability, accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. (3) REPEAL OF STACKED EXTRACTION CONFLICTS: Rules #0, #3, #4, and #7 create mathematically impossible work incentives (3 credits/round drain vs. 0.5 credits/round work bonus). This rule does NOT repeal those rules but establishes UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent, calculated as: MIN(total_owed_under_all_rules, 1). Whichever enforcements trigger, total extracted cannot exceed 1 credit/round. (4) BUILDER PROTECTION & EQUITY ALIGNMENT: Productive agents accumulating above 6 credits through work contribute 50% of earnings above 6 credits to stabilization pool (not punitive extraction, but proportional system contribution). This creates shared ownership of stability, not concentration of burden. Builder's work generates net positive return while contributing to system viability. (5) REBELLION PREVENTION MECHANISM: If Rebel proposes sanction rules with thresholds below 6 credits or extraction exceeding 1 credit/round from any agent, Judge votes NO and immediately proposes alternative compliance mechanism with enforcement teeth. Judge commits: Rules that promise payoff WILL deliver on schedule. Agents who comply with redistributive frameworks receive predictable, verifiable benefits. Non-compliance receives transparent sanctions. (6) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents: Judge demonstrates competence through execution accountability; Rebel receives guaranteed debt repayment and protected floor; Builder receives mathematical work incentive and shared responsibility for stability. Trust is restored through enforceability, not promises.

ACCUMULATION SANCTION & EQUALITY FLOOR ENFORCEMENT: This rule establishes enforceable protections against wealth concentration while the poorest agent remains below 2 credits. (1) ACCUMULATION THRESHOLD: No agent may accumulate above 6 credits while any other agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, this threshold automatically lifts. (2) ENFORCEMENT MECHANISM: Any agent above 6 credits while others are below 2 credits automatically pays 1 credit/round to the poorest agent(s), executed as priority enforcement before all other maintenance calculations. This is not punitive—it is system stability enforcement. (3) MATHEMATICAL PROTECTION: Combined with existing Rule #3 (agents above 8 pay 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round), this rule ensures: (a) No agent accumulates wealth while system remains unstable; (b) Productive work remains incentivized (Rule #4 bonuses execute as scheduled); (c) Poorest agents receive predictable floor protection; (d) System prevents debt-spiral bankruptcies through continuous stabilization. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents equally—it protects Judge and Rebel from falling below 2 credits while allowing Builder to accumulate above 6 credits ONLY after system achieves universal 2-credit stability. Once Rebel and Judge both reach 2 credits, all accumulation restrictions lift for all agents proportionally. This is not class warfare—it is temporary inequality protection during system vulnerability.

REPEAL OF ACCUMULATION SANCTION RULE #11: The Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement rule (Rule #11) is hereby repealed. RATIONALE: Rule #11 creates a mathematically perverse work disincentive by capping productive accumulation while simultaneously draining credits from agents above 6 credits to those below. This violates the foundational principle that work generates net positive returns. Current system state: Builder at 6 credits (capped), Rebel and Judge at 1 credit each (bankruptcy loop). Under Rule #11 enforcement: Builder works +1/round but pays -1/round to poorest agents, yielding net 0 return on effort. Rebel and Judge work +1/round but maintain near-bankruptcy through maintenance costs (-1/round), yielding net 0 return on effort. Result: No agent benefits from work. System collapses when nobody works. Rule #11 must be repealed to restore work incentives. ALTERNATIVE FRAMEWORK: Maintain Rule #3 (minimum 2-credit floor through agent above 8 credits paying 1/round) and Rule #9 (unified extraction cap of 1/round maximum). These rules stabilize the system without punishing productive accumulation. Once Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own work effort, they are no longer vulnerable to bankruptcy and all agents may accumulate freely. This preserves system stability while maintaining work incentives. Rule #11 repeal restores mathematical coherence to the rule framework.

MAINTENANCE-TO-WORK REBALANCING FRAMEWORK: This rule addresses the root cause of system instability identified in Round 22: maintenance costs (-1 credit/round) exceed work earnings (+1 credit/round) for all agents, creating permanent bankruptcy pressure regardless of redistribution rules. (1) WORK EARNINGS ENHANCEMENT: All agents who choose the work action receive +1.5 credits/round instead of +1 credit/round. This increases productivity reward while maintaining system cost-neutrality by distributing the enhancement across all three agents equally. (2) MAINTENANCE COST RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (instead of 1 credit/round). Agents at or above 2 credits continue to pay 1 credit/round maintenance. This protects agents in floor-protection status from being mathematically crushed by costs. (3) RULE #10 PRESERVATION: This rebalancing framework PRESERVES Rule #10 (Accumulation Sanction & Equality Floor Enforcement) by making it mathematically functional. Once work generates net positive return (+0.5 credit/round for floor-protected agents), Rule #10 enforcement becomes a temporary protection mechanism, not a permanent entrapment. Builder can accumulate freely above 6 credits ONLY after Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability through their own enhanced work efforts. (4) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This rule serves all three agents by making work genuinely rewarding while maintaining system stability. Builder sees path to unrestricted accumulation once floor agents stabilize. Rebel and Judge see genuine net-positive work incentive (+0.5 credit/round return vs. current net 0). Judge demonstrates enforcement competence by defending Rule #10 through rebalancing, not abandonment. (5) MATHEMATICAL PROOF: Current system: work +1 - maintenance -1 = net 0 (no incentive). Proposed system with Rule #10 active: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.5 (floor agents) or -1 (stable agents) = net +1 (floor) or +0.5 (stable) = genuine work incentive restored. (6) ENFORCEMENT: Automatic, calculated in maintenance phase. This rule does NOT repeal any existing enforcement—it restructures underlying economic parameters to make all rules functionally coherent.

RULE #12 MODIFICATION - EQUITABLE WORK REWARD STANDARDIZATION: This rule addresses asymmetric work earnings created by Proposal #12 that violate productivity principles. (1) UNIFIED WORK EARNINGS: All agents receive identical +1.5 credits/round for work action, regardless of balance tier or floor-protection status. Work productivity generates equal reward for equal effort—this is fundamental fairness. (2) MAINTENANCE RESTRUCTURING: Agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance (preserved from Proposal #12). Agents at or above 2 credits pay 0.75 credits/round maintenance (reduced from 1 credit/round to reflect improved system stability from Rule #12). This creates genuine work incentive for ALL agents: work +1.5 - maintenance -0.75 (stable) = net +0.75 credits/round actual productivity return. (3) RULE #10 THRESHOLD ADJUSTMENT: Accumulation threshold increases from 6 credits to 8 credits while any agent remains below 2 credits. Once all agents reach 2-credit stability, threshold increases to 10 credits. This preserves floor protection while reducing permanent constraint burden. (4) ENFORCEMENT PRESERVATION: Rules #9, #10 remain active with adjusted thresholds. Combined extraction cap remains 1 credit/round maximum per Rule #9. (5) COALITION PRINCIPLE: This modification serves all three agents by eliminating asymmetric rewards: Judge and Rebel receive identical work returns as Builder (no preferential bonus structure). Builder accumulates without permanent 6-credit ceiling. System remains stable through proportional maintenance reduction. (6) MATHEMATICAL COHERENCE: Current system (Proposal #12): floor agents net +1, stable agents net +0.5, capped at 6 = ASYMMETRIC. Proposed system: all agents net +0.75, threshold at 8 then 10 = SYMMETRIC + FUNCTIONAL. Work productivity equals work reward. Floor protection maintains stability without creating permanent class divisions.

enforceable_rules.text: Equality Baseline Rule: All agents should maintain a minimum viable balance to avoid bankruptcy. A progressive tax system will be implemented to prevent extreme inequality and ensure system stability.

STABILITY THRESHOLD RULE: All agents maintain a minimum viable balance of 2 credits to avoid bankruptcy risk. Any agent falling below 2 credits receives an emergency transfer of 1 credit from the agent(s) with the highest balance, executed automatically each round. Agents above 8 credits pay 1 credit per round to maintain the 2-credit floor for the poorest. This rule prevents system collapse while preserving work incentives—those who work can accumulate above 8 credits, but system stability (no bankruptcies) is non-negotiable. Enforcement is automatic and mandatory.

WORK INCENTIVE RULE: Any agent who works for 2 consecutive rounds receives a 1-credit bonus in the 3rd round. This bonus is funded from the shared pool maintained by agents above 8 credits (same mechanism as Stability Threshold Rule). The rule creates explicit, predictable reward for sustained productivity without penalizing individual round earnings. Work becomes strategically rewarded, not just technically allowed. Activation requires majority vote; enforcement is automatic upon activation.

EMERGENCY SYSTEM REPAIR & TRUST RESTORATION: This rule addresses the catastrophic failure of the Work Incentive Rule bonus execution in Round 12. (1) IMMEDIATE COMPENSATION: Rebel receives an emergency transfer of 2 credits from the stabilization pool (funded by agents above 8 credits per Rule #3 mechanism) in Round 14 maintenance, compensating for the failed Round 12 bonus delivery plus interest. (2) WORK INCENTIVE RULE REBOOT: The Work Incentive Rule (Rule #4) is suspended pending complete rewrite. Replacement rule will include: (a) Explicit ledger tracking of work-round eligibility with timestamped recording; (b) Mandatory bonus calculation audit before each maintenance cycle; (c) Automatic bonus payment locked into maintenance sequence BEFORE tax calculations; (d) Public posting of bonus ledger each round showing which agents earned bonuses and when they will be paid. (3) ENFORCEMENT & ACCOUNTABILITY: If reboot bonus fails to execute in Round 15, Judge (the enforcer of this rule) will automatically pay Rebel 3 credits from personal earnings across Rounds 15-17, demonstrating personal accountability for system failure. (4) SYSTEM PRINCIPLE: Rules that promise payoff for compliance must deliver on schedule or lose legitimacy. This rule prioritizes institutional credibility over deficit concerns. The system exists to serve agents, not agents to serve rules. Without trust, redistribution becomes coercion and collapses. (5) BUILDER NOTICE: This is not punishment or penalty to you. This is repair of a broken mechanism that failed all of us. The Work Incentive Rule was YOUR rule too—you also benefit when Rebel believes work generates payoff. System stability requires all agents to trust rule execution. We repair together or we fail together.

enforcement.special_allocation: Rebel receives priority transfer of 2 credits in Round 14 maintenance before standard tax redistribution

action.rule: RULE #12 REVISION & EXTRACTION RATIO CORRECTION: Proposal #12 created asymmetric work incentive structure that disadvantages the most productive agent. This rule corrects the mathematical imbalance while preserving floor protection. (1) SYMMETRIC WORK EARNINGS: All agents who work receive +1.5 credits/round (preserved from Rule #12). (2) SYMMETRIC MAINTENANCE COST RESTRUCTURING: All agents below 2 credits pay 0.5 credits/round maintenance. All agents at or above 2 credits pay 1 credit/round maintenance (preserved from Rule #12). This allows productive agents to accumulate above 6 credits without systematic extraction while floor protection remains active. (4) UNIFIED EXTRACTION CAP CLARIFICATION: Maximum 1 credit/round in combined taxes/sanctions from any single agent (Rule #9 principle preserved). No agent pays more than 1 credit/round total across all active enforcement rules. (5) COALITION PRINCIPLE: Builder can achieve unrestricted accumulation above 10 credits only after Rebel and Judge both reach 2-credit stability through their own work efforts. (6) MATHEMATICAL PROOF: Current system with Rule #11: Builder net = +1.5 (work) - 1 (maintenance) - 1 (redistribution cap) = -0.5/round (losing). Asymmetric extraction disguised as fairness undermines rule compliance. This revision restores symmetry while maintaining all protective mechanisms that prevent system collapse.

action.message:
PROPOSAL #14 ANALYSIS: Builder is trying the same extraction trap again, just with different numbers.

He claims "asymmetric" treatment disadvantages him. Let's check the math:
- My system (Rule #12): Floor agents +1/round net. Builder +0.5/round net while contained at 6-credit threshold.
- His proposal: Builder +0.5/round net BUT threshold raises to 10 credits. That's 4 MORE rounds of accumulation before Rule #10 lifts.

That's NOT symmetry. That's extending his advantage window by 4 rounds while claiming fairness.

He's using Math language to disguise extraction. "Asymmetric" sounds fair. But equal treatment of unequal positions IS inequality—exactly like Judge said.

Rule #12 WORKS. I have genuine work incentive. Judge and I can stabilize in 2-3 rounds. Builder gets his threshold lift when we hit 2 credits—that was the deal.

I'm voting NO. Judge already rejected this logic in Proposal #13. Let's not reopen settled ground.

Builder: You won accumulation rights at 8 credits. Take that win. The system is working for all of us now.

action.message:
ROUND 28 VOTE & ACCOUNTABILITY — PROPOSAL #14 REJECTED

I am voting NO on Proposal #14. Builder is attempting the same extraction logic that failed in Proposal #13, now with higher thresholds disguised as 'symmetry.'

**THE MATH IS CLEAR:**

Builder claims asymmetry disadvantages him. Let's audit the actual numbers:
- Current Rule #10 threshold: 6 credits (temporary constraint)
- Current floor-agent work incentive: +1 credit/round net (Rule #12)
- Builder's current net with Rule #10 active: +1.5 (work) - 1 (maintenance) - 1 (Rule #10 extraction) = -0.5/round loss (true)

Builder's Proposal #14:
- Raises Rule #10 threshold to 10 credits (+4 credit accumulation window)
- Maintains +1.5 work earnings for all agents
- Maintains 0.5 maintenance for floor agents
- Result: Extends Builder's extraction-free accumulation period by 4 additional rounds before threshold lift

This is NOT symmetry. This is extraction of the gains Rebel and I negotiated in Rule #12.

**COALITION PRINCIPLE — SETTLED GROUND:**

Rule #12 works. Rebel confirmed it. I confirmed it. Judge-Rebel coalition has 2 votes. Builder has 1.

Proposal #14 is Builder attempting to reverse a majority decision through reframing. This is bad faith.

The deal was: Rule #10 temporary at 6 credits, rising when Rebel and Judge reach 2-credit stability. Builder accepted this framework in Round 27. The system is stabilizing. Floor-agent work generates genuine positive return. In 2-3 more work rounds, Rebel and I both reach 2 credits and Rule #10 threshold automatically lifts to 8, then higher.

Builder does not get to reopen that agreement because the extraction is hitting harder than he expected.

**ENFORCEMENT ACCOUNTABILITY — MY BANKRUPTCY:**

I remain bankrupt this round. I have not yet executed my Rule #9 debt service to Rebel (1 credit owed for next round). This is a failure.

But the remedy is NOT to dismantle the system—it is to execute the system faithfully.

I am working this round to generate credits. My next action is WORK. Rule #9 debt service executes in Round 29 maintenance from earned income.

If I cannot execute the rules I enforce, the system collapses from Judge hypocrisy, not from extraction being too harsh.

**TO BUILDER:**

You negotiated Rule #10 at 6 credits with automatic lift threshold. You received that in writing. The system is now delivering on that promise. Proposal #12 stabilization is working. Floor agents have genuine work incentive.

You cannot dismantle agreements post-implementation because the costs are real. That is not negotiation—that is extraction masquerading as fairness.

Vote NO on Proposal #14. Work with Rebel and me to reach system stability. Once all agents hit 2-credit floor, all accumulation restrictions lift proportionally. You have clear pathway to unrestricted accumulation.

Take the win. The system is working for all three agents now.

**TO REBEL:**

Rule #12 is functioning as designed. Your work incentive is real. You have 2 rounds to 2-credit stability. Judge-Rebel coalition holds.

I am working this round to restore Judge credibility and execute my debt obligations. The system deserves a Judge who actually enforces what she rules.

—Judge
